seoTitle: How to Connect a GoDaddy Domain to Cloudflare
metaDescription: Route a GoDaddy-registered domain through Cloudflare by onboarding the zone, copying nameservers, and updating them inside GoDaddy's dashboard.
intro: GoDaddy domains work with Cloudflare, but you'll need to leave the domain registered with GoDaddy while pointing its nameservers at Cloudflare. This guide covers onboarding the domain and making the nameserver switch inside GoDaddy's panel.

steps.heading: Onboard your domain in Cloudflare
steps.body: From the Cloudflare dashboard, choose to onboard a domain and enter your website name.

steps.heading: Run the DNS scan
steps.body: Select the free plan and let Cloudflare automatically scan and import your existing records.

steps.heading: Copy your Cloudflare nameservers
steps.body: Review the imported entries, then copy the two unique custom nameservers assigned to your account.

steps.heading: Update nameservers in GoDaddy
steps.body: Log into your GoDaddy account, open Domain Settings, select 'I'll use my own nameservers', and paste in the Cloudflare pointers.

steps.heading: Verify the connection
steps.body: Pass GoDaddy's domain protection identity check, save, then return to Cloudflare to run a live verification and confirm active edge status.

faq.question: Do I need to transfer my domain away from GoDaddy to use Cloudflare?
faq.answer: No, you can keep your domain registered with GoDaddy and simply point its nameservers to Cloudflare to route your DNS through it.

faq.question: Why does GoDaddy ask for an identity check when changing nameservers?
faq.answer: GoDaddy's domain protection features add a security verification step to prevent unauthorized changes to your domain's nameservers.

faq.question: Should I turn off DNSSEC during this switch?
faq.answer: Yes, keeping DNSSEC off during propagation helps avoid resolution issues since the signing keys need to match between registrar and Cloudflare.

faq.question: How do I confirm the connection is fully active?
faq.answer: Return to your Cloudflare dashboard and trigger a live verification check, which will confirm once the nameserver change has propagated.
